# MaestroStack

> MaestroStack is a config-driven, open source TypeScript CLI that runs Maestro
> mobile tests on BrowserStack App Automate real devices. It packages your Maestro
> flow folders, uploads the app and test suite to BrowserStack, and triggers
> Maestro builds across one or more real devices from a single command:
> `maestrostack run`.

Key facts:
- Install: `npm install -g maestrostack` (Node.js 18+, works on macOS, Linux and Windows).
- License: MIT. Package name on npm: `maestrostack`.
- Commands: `init`, `validate`, `package`, `upload-app`, `upload-suite`, `run`.
- Configuration is a single `maestrostack.yml` file with `auth`, `platform`
  (android or ios), `app`, `suite`, and `run` sections.
- Credentials (`BROWSERSTACK_USERNAME`, `BROWSERSTACK_ACCESS_KEY`) come from the
  environment or a `.env` file and are never printed.
- Execute modes: `explicit` (list flows in `run.execute`) or `main` (a `main.yaml`
  at the suite root).
- Parallel execution: `run.maxParallel` caps how many listed devices run at once;
  MaestroStack submits sequential batched builds and waits for each to finish.
- `maestrostack run --dry-run` previews the files and BrowserStack payload without
  making any API calls.

## Docs

- [Documentation](https://rohanimmanuel.github.io/maestrostack/docs.html): install, configuration, commands, execute modes, parallel execution, FAQ.
- [Source on GitHub](https://github.com/RohanImmanuel/maestrostack)
- [npm package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/maestrostack)
